summary.intent: Implement task #242: build the framework-agnostic TagsInput state machine component for the ars-ui project, including machine logic, ARIA attributes, tests, and spec amendments.
summary.outcome: Delivered a complete TagsInput agnostic core (state machine, 100 unit/snapshot tests, proptest with 3000 cases, spec-conformance test, 97.5% line coverage) with 6 spec drift items resolved and full spec sync; rebased onto main and ready for PR.

repo: Agnostic components in ars-components must never call use_platform_effects() — that is adapter-only. The canonical pattern (per pin_input) is a typed Effect enum resolved by the adapter via ctx.
Vec<String>: BindableValue and &'static str: EffectName are both blanket impls — string effect names from specs are valid at the type level but the codebase convention uses enums.
Controlled-mode Bindable: set() does not reflect in get(). Mutation tests must use uncontrolled default_value, not .value(...) controlled bindings.
KeyboardEventData::named() does not exist — that method is on Hotkey. KeyboardEventData must be constructed manually (matching how checkbox tests do it).
insta --unreferenced=reject must be run with --features i18n to avoid false positives; highlight component snapshot tests are i18n-gated and appear as orphans without the feature.
doc-test # Examples blocks are absent from all selection components (combobox, select, listbox, segment_group) — writing none is the correct convention.
The spec has a cargo xtask spec compile-snippets gate that parses Rust code blocks in markdown; all spec code must be syntactically valid.

Intra-doc links to enum variants and struct fields (e.g., Effect::FocusTag, Context::editing_draft) do not resolve in module-level //! docs even when they resolve in item-level docs; use type links + code spans instead.
cargo xci-fast is the pre-push integration gate; it is not run as part of the local implementation workflow.
The §6.2 parity table in specs tends to use stale part names (Item* naming) that diverge from actual implementation anatomy — always cross-check during spec sync.

code.finding: Dead code guard: !props.editable check inside CommitEdit was unreachable because EditingTag state requires editable at entry and props are immutable. Removed.
Module-level //! intra-doc links to variant/field refs (Effect::FocusTag, Context::editing_draft) emit rustdoc warnings; must use type-only links ([Effect]) plus backtick code spans for variants.
Canonical pattern for Api structs containing fn fields: add a manual Debug impl (same as text_field does) since #[derive(Debug)] cannot be used on function pointer fields.

Proptest discovered that list-mutating events (Remove, ClearAll) during EditingTag state left editing_tag index dangling. Fix: guard CommitEdit/CancelEdit to no-op outside EditingTag; ignore list-mutating events while editing.

workflow: Proptest is essential for state machine components — it found a real invariant violation (dangling edit index) that the 50 hand-written unit tests completely missed.
Running coverage before the post-implementation audit (not after) avoids a two-pass cycle; the audit's Phase 3 specifically checks coverage and adds targeted tests.

The post-implementation audit runs in three phases: (1) spec/impl drift, (2) two consecutive clean surface rounds (adapters, manifest, catalog, doc-tests), (3) coverage with annotated uncovered lines — each phase gates the next.
When clippy -D warnings is run workspace-wide, pre-existing warnings in other crates surface and must be distinguished from new ones introduced by the change.
Spec amendments are in-scope for the same PR as the implementation under the spec-sync rule — they are not deferred to a follow-up.

friction: Spec had 6 architectural drift issues that required reconciliation before implementation could begin: duplicate method definitions, missing editing_draft from Context, use_platform_effects() calls in agnostic code, string effect names vs typed enums, missing delimiter-on-type logic, and missing LiveRegion part.
Closure type inference required explicit &mut Context annotations on helper closures after the initial compile failed with 'type parameter M must be constrained'.
Cross-worktree paths in lcov --text output made it impossible to grep for the tags_input section by filename; required parsing the lcov format directly.

Initially constructed test data using controlled .value(...) bindings, causing all mutation tests to silently pass without state changes — took a full test run to identify the Bindable semantics mismatch.
Multiple rounds of clippy semicolon warnings in test functions required several targeted edit passes.
insta snapshot acceptance required running with i18n feature to avoid false unreferenced-snapshot failures from an unrelated highlight component.
open_items: Adapter components (Leptos/Dioxus) for TagsInput are out of scope for this PR and must be implemented separately.
Widget examples and E2E tests for TagsInput are deferred to a follow-up task.
cargo xci-fast pre-push gate was not run during implementation and must be run before merging the PR.
Pre-existing broken intra-doc links in table/grid components (focused_cell, ColumnResize, sort_descriptor) were identified but not fixed — out of scope for this task.
